heading: Tax on reversion of qualified plan assets to employer
label: 4980 Tax on reversion of qualified plan assets to employer
notes: Amendments2008—Subsec. (c)(2)(B)(iii). added cl. (iii).Pub. L. 110–4582006—Subsec. (c)(3)(A). substituted “if the requirements of subparagraphs (B), (C), and (D) are met” for “if—Pub. L. 109–280“(i) the requirements of subparagraphs (B), (C), and (D) are met, and“(ii) under the plan, employer securities to which subparagraph (B) applies must, except to the extent necessary to meet the requirements of section 401(a)(28), remain in the plan until distribution to participants in accordance with the provisions of such plan”.1996—Subsecs. (a), (d). provided that, except as otherwise expressly provided, whenever in title XII of an amendment or repeal is expressed in terms of an amendment to, or repeal of, a section or other provision, the reference shall be considered to be made to a section or other provision of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986. Effective Date of 1990 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–508, title XII, § 12003104 Stat. 1388–566Nov. 5, 1990“(a).—In GeneralExcept as provided in subsection (b), the amendments made by this subtitle [subtitle A (§§ 12001–12003) of title XII of , amending this section and sections 1002, 1104, and 1344 of Title 29, Labor] shall apply to reversions occurring after .Pub. L. 101–508September 30, 1990“(b).—ExceptionThe amendments made by this subtitle shall not apply to any reversion after , if—September 30, 1990“(1)in the case of plans subject to title IV of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [ et seq.], a notice of intent to terminate under such title was provided to participants (or if no participants, to the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation) before ,29 U.S.C. 1301October 1, 1990“(2)in the case of plans subject to title I [ et seq.] (and not to title IV) of such Act, a notice of intent to reduce future accruals under section 204(h) of such Act [] was provided to participants in connection with the termination before ,29 U.S.C. 100129 U.S.C. 1054(h)October 1, 1990“(3)in the case of plans not subject to title I or IV of such Act, a request for a determination letter with respect to the termination was filed with the Secretary of the Treasury or the Secretary’s delegate before , orOctober 1, 1990“(4)in the case of plans not subject to title I or IV of such Act and having only 1 participant, a resolution terminating the plan was adopted by the employer before .”October 1, 1990

Transfer of Excess Assets From Qualified Pension Plan to Welfare Benefit Plan, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–239, title VII, § 7861(b)103 Stat. 2430Dec. 19, 1989“(1)Notwithstanding any other provision of law, in the case of any qualified pension plan and welfare benefit plan described in paragraph (2), the assets of such pension plan in excess of its liabilities may be transferred to such welfare benefit plan upon the termination of such pension plan if such assets are to be used to provide retiree health benefits.“(2)For purposes of paragraph (1), a qualified pension plan and welfare benefit plan are described in this paragraph if—“(A)both such plans are jointly administered pursuant to a collective bargaining agreement between the employer maintaining such plans and one or more employee representatives,“(B)the welfare benefit plan provides retiree health benefits, and“(C)the qualified pension plan has assets in excess of liabilities (determined on a termination basis) and the welfare benefit plan has assets which are less than the present value of the benefits to be provided under the plan (determined as of the time of termination of the pension plan).“(3)For purposes of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, any transfer of assets to which paragraph (1) applies shall be treated as a reversion of such assets to the employer maintaining the plan which is includible in the gross income of such employer and subject to the tax imposed by section 4980 of such Code.”

children.heading: Imposition of tax
children.label: a Imposition of tax
children.text: There is hereby imposed a tax of 20 percent of the amount of any employer reversion from a qualified plan.

children.heading: Liability for tax
children.label: b Liability for tax
children.text: The tax imposed by subsection (a) shall be paid by the employer maintaining the plan.

children.label: A
children.text: a plan maintained by an employer if such employer has, at all times, been exempt from tax under subtitle A, or

children.label: B
children.text: a governmental plan (within the meaning of section 414(d)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “employer reversion” means the amount of cash and the fair market value of other property received (directly or indirectly) by an employer from the qualified plan.

children.label: i
children.text: except as provided in regulations, any amount distributed to or on behalf of any employee (or his beneficiaries) if such amount could have been so distributed before termination of such plan without violating any provision of section 401,

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a multiemployer plan, by reason of mistakes of law or fact or the return of any withdrawal liability payment,

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a plan other than a multiemployer plan, by reason of mistake of fact, or

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of any plan, by reason of the failure of the plan to initially qualify or the failure of contributions to be deductible, or

children.label: iii
children.text: any transfer described in section 420(f)(2)(B)(ii)(II).

children.heading: Exception for employee stock ownership plans
children.label: 3 Exception for employee stock ownership plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If, upon an employer reversion from a qualified plan, any applicable amount is transferred from such plan to an employee stock ownership plan described in section 4975(e)(7) or a tax credit employee stock ownership plan (as described in section 409), such amount shall not be treated as an employer reversion for purposes of this section (or includible in the gross income of the employer) if the requirements of subparagraphs (B), (C), and (D) are met.

children.heading: Investment in employer securities
children.label: B Investment in employer securities
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met if, within 90 days after the transfer (or such longer period as the Secretary may prescribe), the amount transferred is invested in employer securities (as defined in section 409()) or used to repay loans used to purchase such securities.l

children.label: i
children.text: is credited to a suspense account and allocated from such account to accounts of participants no less rapidly than ratably over a period not to exceed 7 years, and

children.label: I
children.text: the annual addition (as determined under section 415(c)) attributable to each such allocation shall not exceed the value of such securities as of the time such securities were credited to such suspense account, and

children.label: II
children.text: no additional employer contributions shall be permitted to an employee stock ownership plan described in subparagraph (A) of the employer before the allocation of such amount.

children.heading: Participants
children.label: D Participants
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met if at least half of the participants in the qualified plan are participants in the employee stock ownership plan (as of the close of the 1st plan year for which an allocation of the securities is required).

children.label: i
children.text: is transferred after , and before , orMarch 31, 1985January 1, 1989

children.label: ii
children.text: is transferred after , pursuant to a termination which occurs after , and before .December 31, 1988March 31, 1985January 1, 1989

children.heading: No credit or deduction allowed
children.label: F No credit or deduction allowed
children.text: No credit or deduction shall be allowed under chapter 1 for any amount transferred to an employee stock ownership plan in a transfer to which this paragraph applies.

children.heading: Amount transferred to include income thereon, etc.
children.label: G Amount transferred to include income thereon, etc.
children.text: The amount transferred shall not be treated as meeting the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C) unless amounts attributable to such amount also meet such requirements.

children.heading: Time for payment of tax
children.label: 4 Time for payment of tax
children.text: For purposes of subtitle F, the time for payment of the tax imposed by subsection (a) shall be the last day of the month following the month in which the employer reversion occurs.

children.heading: Increase in tax for failure to establish replacement plan or increase benefits
children.label: d Increase in tax for failure to establish replacement plan or increase benefits
A
children.text: the employer establishes or maintains a qualified replacement plan, or

children.label: B
children.text: the plan provides benefit increases meeting the requirements of paragraph (3).

children.heading: Participation requirement
children.label: A Participation requirement
children.text: At least 95 percent of the active participants in the terminated plan who remain as employees of the employer after the termination are active participants in the replacement plan.

children.label: I
children.text: 25 percent of the maximum amount which the employer could receive as an employer reversion without regard to this subsection, over

children.label: II
children.text: the amount determined under clause (ii).

children.heading: Reduction for increase in benefits
children.label: ii Reduction for increase in benefits
I
children.text: is adopted during the 60-day period ending on the date of termination of the qualified plan, and

children.label: II
children.text: takes effect immediately on the termination date.

children.label: I
children.text: such amount shall not be includible in the gross income of the employer,

children.label: II
children.text: no deduction shall be allowable with respect to such transfer, and

children.label: III
children.text: such transfer shall not be treated as an employer reversion for purposes of this section.

children.label: I
children.text: allocated under the plan to the accounts of participants in the plan year in which the transfer occurs, or

children.label: II
children.text: credited to a suspense account and allocated from such account to accounts of participants no less rapidly than ratably over the 7-plan-year period beginning with the year of the transfer.

children.label: I
children.text: such amount shall be allocated to the accounts of other participants, and

children.label: II
children.text: if any portion of such amount may not be allocated to other participants by reason of any such limitation, shall be allocated to the participant as provided in section 415.

children.heading: Treatment of income
children.label: iii Treatment of income
children.text: Any income on any amount credited to a suspense account under clause (i)(II) shall be allocated to accounts of participants no less rapidly than ratably over the remainder of the period determined under such clause (after application of clause (ii)).

children.label: I
children.text: such amount shall be allocated to the accounts of participants as of such date, except that any amount which may not be allocated by reason of any limitation under section 415 shall be allocated to the accounts of other participants, and

children.label: II
children.text: if any portion of such amount may not be allocated to other participants under subclause (I) by reason of such limitation, such portion shall be treated as an employer reversion to which this section applies.

children.label: i
children.text: have an aggregate present value not less than 20 percent of the maximum amount which the employer could receive as an employer reversion without regard to this subsection, and

children.label: ii
children.text: take effect immediately on the termination date.

children.label: i
children.text: the present value of such participant’s accrued benefit (determined without regard to this subsection), bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate present value of accrued benefits of the terminated plan (as so determined).

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: A Limitations
children.text: A benefit may not be increased under paragraph (2)(B)(ii) or (3)(A), and an amount may not be allocated to a participant under paragraph (2)(C), if such increase or allocation would result in a failure to meet any requirement under section 401(a)(4) or 415.

children.heading: Treatment as employer contributions
children.label: B Treatment as employer contributions
children.text: Any increase in benefits under paragraph (2)(B)(ii) or (3)(A), or any allocation of any amount (or income allocable thereto) to any account under paragraph (2)(C), shall be treated as an annual benefit or annual addition for purposes of section 415.

children.heading: 10-year participation requirement
children.label: C 10-year participation requirement
children.text: Except as provided by the Secretary, section 415(b)(5)(D) shall not apply to any increase in benefits by reason of this subsection to the extent that the application of this subparagraph does not discriminate in favor of highly compensated employees (as defined in section 414(q)).

children.label: i
children.text: is an active participant,

children.label: ii
children.text: is a participant or beneficiary in pay status as of the termination date,

children.label: I
children.text: who has a nonforfeitable right to an accrued benefit under the terminated plan as of the termination date, and

children.label: II
children.text: whose service, which was creditable under the terminated plan, terminated during the period beginning 3 years before the termination date and ending with the date on which the final distribution of assets occurs, or

children.label: iv
children.text: is a beneficiary of a participant described in clause (iii)(II) and has a nonforfeitable right to an accrued benefit under the terminated plan as of the termination date.

children.heading: Present value
children.label: B Present value
children.text: Present value shall be determined as of the termination date and on the same basis as liabilities of the plan are determined on termination.

children.heading: Reallocation of increase
children.label: C Reallocation of increase
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2)(C), if any benefit increase is reduced by reason of the last sentence of paragraph (3)(A)(ii) or paragraph (4), the amount of such reduction shall be allocated to the remaining participants on the same basis as other increases (and shall be treated as meeting any allocation requirement of this subsection).

children.label: i
children.text: 2 or more plans may be treated as 1 plan, or

children.label: ii
children.text: a plan of a successor employer may be taken into account.

children.heading: Special rule for participation requirement
children.label: E Special rule for participation requirement
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (2)(A), all employers treated as 1 employer under section 414(b), (c), (m), or () shall be treated as 1 employer.o

children.heading: Subsection not to apply to employer in bankruptcy
children.label: 6 Subsection not to apply to employer in bankruptcy
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to an employer who, as of the termination date of the qualified plan, is in bankruptcy liquidation under chapter 7 of title 11 of the United States Code or in similar proceedings under State law.
